findings: Privacy policy clearly states lawful basis for processing candidate data
Data subject access request (DSAR) process is documented with a defined response process
Encryption at rest and in transit is confirmed
Vendor references GDPR compliance in their privacy policy
Right to erasure is acknowledged and a deletion request process is available
gaps: No mention of data retention timelines for rejected candidates
Sub-processors list is not publicly disclosed and was not provided upon request
Data breach notification timeline is not explicitly stated — exceeds GDPR 72-hour requirement
Cross-border data transfer mechanisms (SCCs or adequacy decisions) are not referenced
Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is not proactively provided — available only upon request
recommendations: Request vendor to share updated sub-processor list and include disclosure obligation in DPA
Negotiate contractual SLA for breach notification within 48 hours to align with GDPR requirement
Confirm and document data retention and deletion policy for rejected candidate records (recommended: 12 months)
Request clarification on cross-border data transfer mechanisms and obtain SCCs if applicable
Ensure a fully executed GDPR-compliant DPA is in place prior to go-live or contract renewal
notes: This is a sample assessment for illustrative purposes only. Vendor identity has been anonymised. All findings and gaps are based on publicly available documentation reviewed at the time of assessment. Always involve legal and compliance teams for actual vendor risk decisions.
